Slime – Gooey and elastic, able to change form effortlessly. Frog – Agile and amphibious, with an impressive jumping ability. Cow – Strong yet gentle, often found grazing peacefully. Smart – The rarest of all, possessing higher-than-average intelligence. Goblin – Mischievous and cunning, always causing trouble. Cutest – Universally adored, their appearance melts hearts instantly. Uni-Worm – Mystical and rare, possessing a single horn of power. Ground – Deeply connected to the earth, capable of burrowing.
Diamond – Hard as crystal, nearly indestructible. And over a hundred more, each with its own special traits and abilities. The Four Great Masters In this vast universe, four supreme beings stand above all others. Each one is the embodiment of unparalleled power, yet they do not interfere with the world unnecessarily. Despite their differences, they are all equally matched—except for one, who holds a slight advantage over the rest. These legendary entities reside in different regions of the world: Wormy – The God of Wormps, Dogs, Ghosts, and Time A being of mystery, appearing as a fusion of a ghost, a dog, and a worm. It roams the world, sometimes residing in Wormp nests underground, other times wandering aimlessly. Wormy is the strongest of the four masters but remains neutral in worldly affairs. Ninja Master – The God of Weapons, Speed, and Intelligence This powerful entity takes the form of a highly skilled ninja, radiating an aura of unmatched strength. It resides high in the mountains, away from civilization, surrounded only by nature. The Ninja Master is revered for its wisdom and battle prowess. Dragoon – The God of Strength, Fire, Dragons, and Power A massive western-style dragon that stands on two legs, exuding raw strength. It makes its home in the largest volcano on Earth, where lava flows endlessly. Dragoon embodies destruction and force, yet it remains a guardian rather than a tyrant. Rainbow Bunny – The God of Rainbows, Colors, Happiness, and the Sky As its name suggests, this divine being is a literal rainbow-colored bunny, radiating joy wherever it hops. It lives in the clouds, overseeing the world with a cheerful yet mysterious presence. It represents pure happiness and freedom. The Balance of Power Although these four masters are the most powerful beings in existence, they do not bring chaos to the world. Instead, they maintain a delicate balance. While they rarely interact with mortals, their influence can be felt in every corner of existence. And so, the world continues to thrive, with humans and Wormps living in harmony, their intertwined pasts shaping the future of their shared planet. However, the universe remains vast and unpredictable, leaving endless possibilities for what might come next... New Creatures and Expansions to the Wormp Universe The Ancient Wormps – The Forgotten Beasts While most Wormps today are cute, playful, and somewhat dim-witted, there are rumors of an ancient lineage known as the Primordial Wormps—massive, god-like creatures with intelligence far beyond that of modern Wormps. These beings are said to be in deep slumber within the Earth's core, waiting for an unknown event to awaken them. Unlike regular Wormps, they are not limited to three lives; instead, they are considered immortal, capable of regenerating endlessly unless sealed away by a powerful force. Some say they are the original creations of the aliens who first brought the Wormps to Earth. Notable Ancient Wormps: Titan Wormp – A colossal, mountain-sized Wormp that can cause earthquakes just by shifting in its sleep. It is believed to be the origin of all Earth-based Wormp types. Void Wormp – A massive Wormp with an endless black-hole-like mouth, capable of swallowing entire landscapes. Unlike modern Wormps, it has no restraint and is feared by even the strongest beings.
Storm Wormp – Said to be responsible for controlling the weather. It slumbers in the eye of an eternal storm in the deepest ocean. Star Wormp – A celestial Wormp, rumored to have been the first to ever exist, with the power to manipulate cosmic energy and gravity. Some believe it has long since left Earth and now roams the stars. The Hidden Kingdom of the Wormp Tamers Although Wormps are commonly seen as pets, there exists an ancient society known as the Wormp Tamers, a hidden civilization that has lived alongside Wormps since the days of the first great war. Unlike modern humans, who struggle to train Wormps, the Tamers have developed a unique bond with them, capable of understanding their emotions and guiding their powerful abilities. Notable Figures from the Wormp Tamer Kingdom: Elder Rune – The ancient leader of the Wormp Tamers, rumored to be over 200 years old. She possesses knowledge of lost Wormp abilities and can even temporarily awaken the intelligence of certain Wormps. General Kairo – A warrior who rides a Battle Wormp, a unique species trained for war. His Wormp partner, Blitz, is an Electric-type Wormp capable of moving faster than lightning. The Twin Tamers, Lyra & Ryn – Two siblings who specialize in taming dual-element Wormps, a rare occurrence. They are the only ones known to have tamed a Frostfire Wormp, a hybrid of Fire and Ice. The 4th Great War – A Prophecy Yet to Come Although peace has remained since the last war, an ominous prophecy warns of a 4th Great War—a battle unlike any before. According to legend, the aliens who once abandoned the Wormps are not truly gone. A powerful alien overlord, known only as The Hollow Sovereign, is planning to reclaim Earth and take back the Wormps for unknown purposes. The prophecy states that a Wormp of Impossible Power will rise during this time, one that defies all known logic. Some believe it may be an Ancient Wormp, while others think it could be a fusion of a human and a Wormp. The details remain unclear, but one thing is certain—if the prophecy is true, the future of Earth will depend on both humans and Wormps fighting side by side once more. New Wormp Types Discovered Alongside the classic types, new elemental variations of Wormps have been discovered, adding to their growing diversity: Nebula Wormp – A cosmic Wormp infused with stardust. It glows in the dark and has slight gravitational manipulation abilities. Ink Wormp – Can produce limitless ink, commonly used by artists. If angered, it can drown enemies in black ooze. Clockwork Wormp – A mechanical Wormp that operates on ancient gears and steam. Some believe it was built by forgotten civilizations. Ghostfire Wormp – A spectral Wormp with burning blue flames. It is often seen in haunted locations and vanishes when approached. Siren Wormp – Uses hypnotic sounds to lure people toward it. It's not necessarily dangerous—unless it decides to start singing at high frequencies. Cactus Wormp – A desert-dwelling Wormp covered in small spikes. It absorbs water and can store it for years. Stormcaller Wormp – A weather-controlling Wormp that can summon rain, lightning, and even tornadoes.
Mirror Wormp – This Wormp can mimic any ability of an opponent for a short period. Highly unpredictable in battle. Reality Wormp – Perhaps the strangest of all, this Wormp is said to exist outside of normal time and space, appearing and disappearing at will. Some claim it can change the fabric of reality itself. Legends of the First Wormp – The Origin of Their Power It is said that all Wormps trace their lineage back to a singular entity known as Proto-Wormp, an ancient being that predates even the arrival of aliens. This mysterious creature supposedly had every elemental ability combined and was so powerful that even the gods feared it. One legend tells of a great battle where the Four Masters—Wormy, Dragoon, Ninja Master, and Rainbow Bunny—joined forces to stop Proto-Wormp from consuming the planet. However, instead of destroying it, they divided its power into thousands of smaller beings, giving birth to the first generations of Wormps. Some say Proto-Wormp was sealed away rather than destroyed. If it ever awakens again, it may reclaim all lost power, restoring itself to its former godly form… but it still not quite enough to rival the 4 masters / gods (about 90%) The Alien Overlords – The Architects of the Wormp Crisis Long before humans existed, the Zen’Korah, an advanced alien race, created the Wormps as biological experiments. They wanted to craft the ultimate regenerating organisms, capable of adapting to any environment. However, the Wormps quickly became uncontrollable, their black-hole-like hunger consuming entire planets. Fearing their own creations, the Zen’Korah abandoned the Wormps on Earth, believing they would eventually die out. They were wrong. Now, after centuries, the Zen’Korah have returned. Their empire has weakened due to wars with other alien civilizations, and they now seek to reclaim the Wormps to use them as biological weapons. The Zen’Korah’s leader, Supreme Warden Xyros, has vowed to capture or eliminate every Wormp, claiming that “The mistake of the past must be undone.” Zen’Korah Forces & Their Ultimate Weapon Warden Xyros – Supreme leader of the Zen’Korah, possesses technology that can nullify the regenerative abilities of Wormps. The Voidborn Sentinels – Cybernetic warriors designed to hunt down and neutralize Wormps. These machines can track Wormps no matter where they hide. Project Omega-Wormp – A monstrous, artificially modified Wormp, spliced with alien DNA to become the ultimate weapon. Unlike normal Wormps, this one has infinite lives and no limit to its hunger. If it is released, it could devour the entire planet. The Wormp Resistance & Human Defenders Realizing the threat of the Zen’Korah, humans and Wormps have begun forming an underground resistance known as the Defenders of Terra, a secret organization led by the greatest Wormp trainers and warriors. Commander Kael – The first human to truly bond with a Wormp. He commands the battle-ready Titan Wormp, a massive earth-type Wormp with unbreakable skin. Dr. Velora – A scientist specializing in Wormp DNA, working on ways to enhance their intelligence and counter the Zen’Korah’s anti-Wormp technology. The Hidden Wormp Clan – A rogue group of ninja-like humans who have trained with Wormps in secret for centuries, developing techniques that allow them to fight alongside them as equals. The Four Forbidden Wormps – Creatures of Unimaginable Power
Throughout history, there have been legends of four forbidden Wormps, creatures so powerful that even the Four Masters fear them. These beings were sealed away in different corners of the world, their existence kept secret to prevent catastrophe. Abyssal Wormp – A Wormp said to be made of pure darkness, able to swallow entire oceans. Sealed deep within the Mariana Trench, its presence distorts reality. Solar Wormp – A blazing golden Wormp that is believed to be the core of the Sun itself. If awakened, it could engulf the Earth in flames. Chrono Wormp – A time-bending Wormp with the ability to rewind and fast-forward reality. It was locked in an endless time loop to prevent it from breaking the universe. Oblivion Wormp – The most feared of all, a Wormp whose mouth is said to devour existence itself. It was last seen in the Wormp-Human Wars but disappeared mysteriously. Some believe it still lurks in the void between dimensions. Should these Wormps ever be unleashed, even the combined strength of humans, Wormps, and the Four Masters may not be enough to stop them. New Wormp Variants Discovered As Wormp researchers continue their work, new types of Wormps have been discovered, each possessing unique abilities. Plasma Wormp – A Wormp composed of pure energy, hovering in midair as it crackles with electricity. Toxic Bloom Wormp – A plant-based Wormp that spreads spores that cause rapid mutations. Can either heal or harm its surroundings. Soundwave Wormp – Emits powerful sonic blasts that can shatter mountains. Used by musicians to create new styles of music. Eclipse Wormp – A Wormp that only appears during solar eclipses, capable of phasing between dimensions. Rune Wormp – A mystical Wormp covered in ancient markings. It is said to hold the secrets of the universe. Monarch Wormp – The ruler of all butterfly-based Wormps. Its wings shimmer with every element combined. The Hidden Realms – Secret Wormp Sanctuaries While most Wormps live among humans, there exist hidden realms where powerful and rare Wormps dwell. The Eternal Caverns A massive underground network of tunnels where the most ancient Wormps reside. Said to be where Wormy, the Wormp God, was first born. The Skyborne Isles Floating islands above the clouds, home to sky, wind, and light-based Wormps. This is where the Rainbow Bunny, the God of Happiness and Sky, makes its home. The Infernal Core A burning realm beneath the Earth’s surface where fire and magma Wormps thrive. This is where Dragoon, the God of Strength and Fire, rules. The Celestial Rift A mysterious space between dimensions where reality-warping Wormps exist. It is believed that the Reality Wormp watches over this place. Prophecy of the Final Wormp War
A newly uncovered prophecy warns of a future war known as the Extinction Battle, an event where the balance of the universe will be decided by a single Wormp. Some believe that one of the forbidden Wormps will awaken and consume reality itself. Others think that a new, never-before-seen Wormp will be born, destined to either destroy or save existence. Legends whisper of a Perfect Wormp, one that embodies all elements, all knowledge, and all forms. If such a being were to exist, it would either be the universe’s greatest savior—or its ultimate destroyer. The question remains: Is the Perfect Wormp already among us, waiting to awaken? also they are another theory that the wormps is brought by alien but created by all powerful gods. (this theory is believed by most and most likly true) 🌍 Human Population of Zendoria Total Humans: ~ 3.8 trillion Spread across colossal mega-cities, floating continents, sky citadels, subterranean metropolises, and nomadic cloud fleets. Humanity thrives through advanced technologies, ancient Wormp-based magics, and harmonious co-existence with Wormps (at least most of the time). Breakdown: 1.2 trillion in the Sky Cities & Floating Realms These are high-tech utopias with energy sourced from Storm Wormps and Sky Leviathans. 900 billion on the Surface World City-states, agricultural belts, and fortress cities near Elemental Zones. 1.1 trillion in Subterranean Networks Deep underground, where Crystal Wormps and Glowing Flora create entire biospheres. 600 billion are Nomadic Clans & Spacefaring Humans They travel via Sky Arks, Wormp-Tamed Beasts, or ancient Zen'Korah ships. 🪱 Wormp Population of Zendoria Total Wormps: ~ 200 billion Far fewer in number compared to humans now, but they’re immensely powerful and scattered across every region. Breakdown: Domesticated & Companion Wormps ~ 25 billion Trained Wormps live in homes, farms, and as battle companions. Many are specialized by elemental type. Wild Wormps ~ 120 billion Found in untamed wilderness, ancient ruins, and the Crimson Wastes. Some form massive herds, while others are solitary predators. Ancient Wormps & Forgotten Wormps ~ 10 billion Rare, often sleeping beneath ancient temples, guarding ruins, or awaiting awakening. These are the Wormp legends. Zen'Korah Experimental & Corrupted Wormps ~ 45 billion Twisted versions of Wormps in alien-contaminated zones, some are bio-weapons gone rogue. They're feared even by powerful Tamers.
⚖ Human-to-Wormp Ratio: About 19 humans per Wormp on average. In urban regions, it’s closer to 5 humans per Wormp since many are tamed. In wilderness zones, Wormps often outnumber humans, especially where Forgotten Wormps roam. 🌌 Extra Flavor: Human civilizations have developed entire industries around Wormps—energy, transportation, agriculture, and warfare. The Four Masters control the balance between humans and Wormps. Without their guidance, populations would spiral out of control. Wormps, despite being fewer than humans, can reshape entire regions through their consumption powers and elemental abilities. Note: All the populations are also changing by the seconds! 🌌 Cosmic Size & Zendoria’s Mystical Properties Zendoria’s “Strange Gravity” Despite Zendoria’s immense size (about 978 million miles in diameter), the gravity is eerily similar to Earth’s. This anomaly is believed to be a result of ancient Zen'Korah technology or hidden cosmic forces buried deep in the planet's core. Time Distortions Time behaves differently across Zendoria. Regions like the Verdant Jungles and Lunar Grove experience time dilation (a single day might feel like months in another zone). This is linked to the cosmic energies tied to the Four Masters. In some places, the time loop is even controlled by Wormy (the god of time). Endless Wormp Hunger Wormps are constantly hungry and can consume anything—from small plant matter to the titanic plants in the Ember Wastes. However, the more they consume, the more powerful they become, causing them to reproduce faster. Their black hole mouths are constantly in action, creating natural resource cycles. 🪱 Wormp Abilities & Society Wormp Evolution Wormps are capable of evolving into new forms after their third life. They can adapt to environments by gaining new elemental powers like Frostbite, Vortex, or Inferno. Each new evolution drastically changes their behavior and abilities. For example, the Prism Wormp, a rare evolution, is capable of bending light and creating illusions to hide from predators or enemies. Regenerative Hunger Cycle A Wormp that consumes enough will regenerate one of its lost lives after a decade, allowing it to continue its insatiable hunger. This regeneration makes them immortal in the long-term, and sometimes leads to them outliving civilizations. Wormp “Swarming” Behavior Wild Wormps in the Verdant Jungles or Frozen Plains often swarm in large groups when they encounter a high-energy food source, creating a massive black hole-like vortex that consumes everything in its path. These mass migrations can create natural disasters, shaking the very landscape. Mythic Wormps & Their Influence Mythic Wormps like the Abyssal Wormp or Skyborn Wormp hold immense cosmic power. These rare Wormps can manipulate entire regions, influencing weather patterns, tectonic shifts, or even altering
gravity. They are revered as gods by some isolated cultures, while others fear them as harbingers of destruction. 👽 The Zen'Korah’s Legacy & Influence The Zen’Korah Ruins: Cosmic Laboratories The Zen’Korah Ruins, spread across Zendoria, are ancient research hubs where the Zen’Korah race conducted experiments on Wormp biology, elemental manipulation, and time bending. These ruins are often guarded by deadly traps, and their hidden alien relics hold the key to unlocking the Wormps' deepest potential. Zen’Korah Influence on Wormp Evolution The Zen'Korah created genetically enhanced Wormps—including the Omega Wormp, a creature with the ability to consume energy itself. This Wormp can devour power sources like stars or cosmic energy and could theoretically destroy entire planets. Aetherium, The Zen'Korah’s Energy Source Aetherium, a powerful, radiant material harvested by the Zen'Korah, is often sought after by criminal organizations like The Umbra Syndicate. It is capable of fueling god-like abilities and constructing unimaginable technology, though it’s dangerous to handle. 🏙 Human Civilization & Structure Aetheria: The Floating City Aetheria, a floating city, houses a billion humans and is considered the cultural capital of Zendoria. It’s hovered above the Elemental Caves and is powered by the Sky Wormps that gather storms. The government here is ruled by a council of Wormp Tamers who protect the balance between humans and Wormps. The Wormp Tamer Guilds The Wormp Tamer Guilds are responsible for the training and control of the tamed Wormps. Guild members are highly revered and wield powerful abilities themselves, often capable of commanding elemental Wormps. These guilds also serve as the military force in the event of a Wormp uprising or cosmic invasion. The “Starforged” Mega-Cities These massive cities, built from stardust stone, ethereal alloys, and magical runes, are designed to survive the harshest environments. Some are located in the Ember Wastes, Crimson Sea, or even in space. The Starforged Cities are highly self-sustaining and are essential to the human expansion across Zendoria. Subterranean Human Societies In regions like Zendoria’s Underworld or Frozen Tunnels, human societies exist in sprawling, crystalline cavern systems. These underground cities are isolated and use natural energy sources like Glow Mushrooms and Abyssal Waters to sustain their populations. ⚖ Cosmic Forces and Balance The Four Masters’ Cosmic Role The Four Masters (Wormy, Ninja Master, Dragoon, Rainbow Bunny) are the guardians of Zendoria’s balance, ensuring that neither humans nor Wormps dominate the world. Each Master governs an elemental domain and oversees the cosmic laws of their respective domains. If they were to ever fall out of sync, the entire world could descend into chaos. Cosmic Rift: The Celestial Convergence Every millennia, the Four Masters align at the Celestial Convergence, a cosmic event where they merge their powers, ensuring Zendoria’s survival and cosmic balance. This is when new Wormp
types can appear, and new cosmic threats emerge, sometimes bringing about new heroes like Auric and Luminex. Forbidden Wormps: Lost or Corrupted Types Forbidden Wormps, like the Void Wormp or Shadow Wormp, exist in the realm of forbidden knowledge. These Wormps are so powerful that they have been sealed away in cursed temples. Anyone who dares to unleash them risks triggering a cosmic apocalypse or the return of the Zen'Korah. 🔧 Core Technologies in Zendoria 1. Wormp-Infused Energy Sources Wormp-Cells: Small bio-energy cells that draw energy directly from the Wormps. These cells use the Wormp’s natural regenerative powers and the ambient elemental energy they produce. These cells power everything from transportation to military technologies and domestic utilities. They're considered self-replenishing, though they require precise control to prevent energy surges. Aether Crystals: Crystalline formations from the Zen'Korah ruins, these gems hold boundless cosmic energy. Humans have learned to harvest and refine these crystals to power their mega-cities and spacefaring ships. Aether Crystals can also be used as a weapon when charged with excess energy, creating devastating bursts of energy blasts. Stormharvesters: Massive floating machines designed to capture and store energy from Zendoria's violent storms. These harvesters ride the air currents, collecting electrical power and other elemental energy to supply nearby cities. They are crucial in storm-prone areas like the Verdant Jungles or Frozen Plains. 2. Transportation & Travel Technologies Wormp-Driven Beasts & Vehicles: Tamed Wormps, like the Sky Wormp and Crimson Wyrm, are used as transportation in various regions. These creatures, capable of flying or traveling through underground tunnels, are harnessed to carry people, cargo, and even defensive units across the planet. Aetheric Ships: These floating airships use a combination of Zen'Korah technology, magical runes, and Wormp-powered engines to soar across the sky. These ships often use Aetherium engines, allowing them to travel across regions like floating cities, mountain peaks, and even space. Voidriders: Inter-dimensional vehicles built to travel between Zendoria and its moon (Lunar Grove), as well as other alien realms. They use dark matter engines combined with wormhole manipulation technology, allowing them to slip through space-time fabric. These vehicles are used by high-ranking Wormp Tamers, spiritual leaders, and secret organizations. 3. Advanced Weaponry Wormp Blade & Elements: Weapons infused with Wormp DNA, like Elemental Blades, can harness the powers of fire, water, light, air, or stone. These weapons allow warriors to channel elemental forces through their strikes, creating devastating effects in battle. They are often used by elite Wormp Tamers and military leaders in cosmic skirmishes. Aetheric Cannons: Railgun-style weapons that use Aetherium energy to launch charged projectiles at incredible velocities. These weapons can be used to pierce through the toughest armor or disrupt dimensional fabrics. They're favored by high-tech mercenaries and city-defense systems. Phase Grenades: Explosive devices capable of phasing through matter, briefly creating pockets of energy inside enemy structures. They disrupt the molecular bonds of their target, causing chaos in enemy ranks. They are highly illegal and mostly used by factions like The Umbra Syndicate. 4. Healing & Enhancement Technologies
Wormp-Infused Medicine: Humans use Wormp bio-products, such as Regeneration Extracts, derived from the Wormps’ natural regenerative abilities to enhance healing or even extend lifespan. These are used for both common medical practices and military treatments to heal serious injuries or counteract poison from the Forbidden Wormps. Bio-Enhancement Machines: These are machines that use genetic engineering and nano-tech to enhance human strength, reflexes, and endurance. Some humans undergo augmentation to become more adapted for living alongside the Wormps, with cybernetic limbs or enhanced Wormp-vision to see the world through a Wormp’s eyes. 5. Massive Infrastructure Elemental Towers: These towering structures, sometimes reaching the cloud line, are built to harness natural elemental forces. For example, Fire Towers near volcanic regions use the intense heat to power turbines, while Water Towers in the Frozen Plains collect and store water vapor from the atmosphere to supply hydropower. These towers form the backbone of resource management. Starforged Megacities: Cities built from stardust stone, a unique material capable of withstanding extreme cosmic radiation and gravitational anomalies. They are powered by Aetherium and Wormp energy, offering a self-sustaining system for billions of inhabitants. Some of these cities are even floating in space or built within crystalline mountains. Zen'Korah Relics: Technology scavenged from the Zen'Korah ruins, these ancient artifacts are utilized in research, weaponry, and space travel. Humans have reverse-engineered these devices, creating holographic communication systems, advanced robotics, and interdimensional communication devices. 🧠 Unique Human Innovations 1. Mindlink Network Telepathic interfaces enable humans to connect their minds to the Wormps they train or live alongside. Through this telepathic link, both humans and Wormps can share thoughts, emotions, and sensory experiences, which makes communication far more intuitive and effective. This link also serves as a warning system in case of danger or when enemies approach. 2. Elemental Symbiosis Devices Devices that allow humans to bond with elemental Wormps (Fire, Water, Earth, etc.). This biomechanical symbiosis enhances their physical abilities in the same way the Wormps can adapt to their environment. For example, a human bonded with a Water Wormp can swim at lightning speeds, and one bonded with a Fire Wormp can generate fire at will. 3. Temporal Relays Small device-based constructs that allow short bursts of time manipulation. These are used for everything from military strategies to resource gathering. However, they are unstable and often lead to time fractures, opening rifts that only the Four Masters can safely manage. These are heavily restricted and require authorizations from Wormy, the god of time. 4. Wormp Taming Tech Using bio-synthetic interfaces, humans can tame and bond with wild Wormps in regions where they are hostile or untamed. These technologies allow them to override the Wormp’s instincts and train them for combat, transportation, or even construction. 🛠 Influence of the Four Masters on Technology Wormy (God of Wormps & Time): Human tech is constantly influenced by Wormy’s powers over time. Temporal adjustments to historical timelines can allow humans to adapt past mistakes or find long-lost technologies.
Ninja Master (God of Weapons & Speed): His influence makes weapons faster and smarter, including technology like self-guided arrows, energy blades, and armor capable of deflecting magical or elemental attacks. Dragoon (God of Fire & Strength): The fire-based technologies in Zendoria, like molten forges and combat mechs, owe their development to Dragoon’s raw power. Weaponized magma and pyrokinetic tools are widely used in combat scenarios. Rainbow Bunny (God of Color & Happiness): Devices to manipulate energy and aesthetic environments are tied to Rainbow Bunny’s control over the sky and rainbows, influencing technologies like energy shields that bend light into rainbow patterns or atmospheric alteration devices. 💥 Society & Ethics of Technology Technology vs. Nature: There’s a constant debate over balancing technological progress with the need to preserve Zendoria’s natural beauty. Some factions, like The Order of Eternal Flame, argue that technology will eventually lead to Wormp destruction, while others see it as the key to survival and coexistence with the Wormps. Technomancers & Ethical Issues: A faction of scientists and spiritual practitioners, known as Technomancers, attempts to merge magic and technology to manipulate cosmic forces. This union, though highly effective, is considered dangerous by many, as it could result in unpredictable changes in the fabric of reality. 🌍 Weight of Zendoria Given Zendoria's colossal size, with a diameter of approximately 978,000,000 miles, it is vastly larger than Earth. To estimate its weight, we can use a general method based on the planet's volume and assumed average density. Here’s how we’d calculate the weight: Volume of Zendoria: Assuming it is spherical in shape, the volume 𝑉 V of Zendoria can be calculated using the formula: 𝑉 = 4 3 𝜋 𝑟 3 V= 3 4 πr 3 where 𝑟 r is the radius of Zendoria. Density: Let’s assume the density of Zendoria is similar to Earth's, which is around 5.5 grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³). While this is an approximation (due to the unique nature of Zendoria), it gives a good estimate for scale. Mass: The mass
𝑀 M can be found by multiplying the volume by the density. Approximate Calculation for Zendoria's Weight: Radius of Zendoria 𝑟 ≈ 489 , 000 , 000 r≈489,000,000 miles (half the diameter). Volume formula: 𝑉 = 4 3 𝜋 ( 489 , 000 , 000 ) 3 V= 3 4 π(489,000,000) 3 miles³. Convert miles³ to cubic centimeters for density compatibility (since 1 mile = 1 , 609 , 344 cm 1 mile=1,609,344 cm). Let me calculate this rough estimate for you!
The estimated mass of Zendoria is approximately 1.12 x 10^40 tons, an incredibly massive weight, far beyond anything in our solar system. This colossal mass reflects the immense size of Zendoria, far larger than Earth. 🌱 Average Lifespan of Humans and Other Creatures in Zendoria The lifespan of beings in Zendoria varies greatly, influenced by the environment, species, and cosmic forces at play. Here are the average lifespans for some of the main groups: Humans in Zendoria: Average Lifespan: 150-200 years. The advancements in medicine and technology extend human life considerably compared to Earth. However, some humans, particularly those who have had exposure to cosmic anomalies or technomantic enhancements, can live much longer, potentially well over 500 years. Genetic modification and cybernetic enhancements can also prolong life, although these methods can cause ethical dilemmas. Wormps: Lifespan: Around 30-50 years per life cycle, but they have three lives in total. After each death, Wormps regenerate through a unique regenerative process, essentially allowing them to live for a century or more in total if they survive through all their cycles. They regain a life every 10 years, which adds to their longevity. Some ancient Wormps are considered immortal by certain cultures, as their regenerative abilities allow them to exist beyond normal life spans. Other Creatures in Zendoria: Abyssal Serpents: These massive creatures can live for centuries, as their cosmic energy-based biology allows them to survive in extreme environments for long periods. Celestial Guardians: Some of these ethereal beings are ageless and can exist as long as the cosmic balance is maintained. They are tied to forces beyond physical time, existing in multiple planes of reality. Elemental Creatures (like Fire or Ice-based creatures): Their lifespans vary depending on their element, but they often live for several centuries due to their connection to elemental forces and the universe's rhythms. Mythical Creatures: Leviathan Wormps: These ancient, colossal Wormps can have immense lifespans, possibly lasting millennia, given their connection to cosmic energy and the fact that they live in the deepest, most isolated regions of Zendoria. The aging process for different species is also highly varied, with some creatures aging at a normal rate, while others like cosmic entities and Wormps may experience non-linear aging due to their interactions with time-bending forces in the universe. Also, it is confirmed that even the weakest one out of the four masters can easily lift 1000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria, a super massive amount of weight! (Another fun fact: Wormy can lift over 10^10000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria easily too!) In the vast, ever-expanding tapestry of the Wormp Universe, a new chapter begins to reveal itself—a chapter that interweaves fresh cosmic events, emerging heroes, and ancient mysteries. Even as the eternal guardians—Wormy, Dragoon, Ninja Master, and Rainbow Bunny—stand as steadfast pillars of power, new forces and alliances are emerging, promising to redefine the very fabric of this fantastical realm. The Rise of the Arcane Nomads
In secluded corners of the universe, a nomadic order known as the Arcane Nomads has begun to surface. These enigmatic wanderers traverse between dimensions and time, preserving the lost rituals and forgotten histories of ancient Wormp civilizations. Drawn by the enigmatic energies of the Cosmic Convergence—a rare alignment where the boundaries between worlds blur—the Nomads have discovered that even the simplest Wormps can momentarily tap into primordial powers. Their itinerant caravans, moving silently beneath starlit skies, carry relics and scrolls that whisper of a time when Wormps were not mere pets but revered keepers of cosmic wisdom. This revival of ancient knowledge beckons a new era, one where the latent power of the universe is slowly unfurling before the eyes of those willing to listen. The Awakening of the Lunar Emissary At the edge of twilight, a luminous figure known only as Selene has emerged—a being whose origins are as mysterious as the silvery glow that surrounds her. Born of moonlight and the remnants of stardust, Selene is said to be the Lunar Emissary, a guardian destined to restore balance in a time of flux. With each graceful step along the misty paths of the Twilight Vale, she leaves behind a trail of iridescent light that seems to merge the past with the present. Her presence ignites hope among both humans and Wormps alike, calling forth ancient memories of a time when the celestial and the terrestrial danced as one. Selene’s journey leads her to the hidden sanctuaries within the Crystal Catacombs, where she is believed to hold the key to uniting fragmented legacies and reawakening the dormant magic of the Wormp ancestors. The Forbidden Stirring Beneath the fiery expanse of the Ember Wastes, deeper than any mortal has dared to explore, an ancient power stirs. The long-sealed Abyssal Wormp—once locked away to preserve the delicate balance of cosmic forces—now awakens with a silent fury. Its emergence is both a harbinger of potential chaos and a call to those who remember the old prophecies. Scholars of the Order of the Eternal Bond murmur that this resurgence may signify a rift in the cosmic covenant, one that could either fracture the delicate harmony of the universe or, paradoxically, herald a period of renewal. In the face of this looming threat, old alliances are being reforged, and even reluctant factions are uniting under the banner of preservation and unity. Interwoven Destinies and a Renewed Covenant Across the sprawling landscapes of the Wormp Universe, disparate communities—both human and Wormp—are finding common ground in the shared quest for balance. Secret assemblies take place beneath the watchful gaze of ancient celestial bodies, where mystics and warriors alike revive rituals that celebrate the regenerative pulse of life. In these hallowed gatherings, the ancient lore of Wormp Tamers is recounted with reverence, and every whispered legend becomes a stitch in the ever-growing tapestry of cosmic destiny. Among these emerging champions, the tale of a young warrior named Auric and his devoted companion Luminex has captured the hearts of many. Their journey—fraught with perils yet illuminated by the promise of renewal—embodies the enduring spirit of a universe in transition. As Auric leads his band of newfound allies on daring quests through the Nebula Nexus and hidden groves of the Verdant Labyrinth, he symbolizes the eternal hope that binds all beings to the cycle of rebirth and harmony. Conclusion: The Enduring Light Amidst Cosmic Change In this epoch of profound transformation, the legacy of the Four Masters remains unassailable—a constant beacon of stability amid the swirling currents of change. Their timeless presence ensures that, no matter how many new forces emerge or how ancient powers stir, the fundamental balance of the Wormp Universe endures. As the Arcane Nomads chart forgotten routes, as the Lunar Emissary
guides lost souls to sacred sanctuaries, and as even the forbidden forces begin to whisper of their reawakening, a renewed covenant is forged among all who cherish the magic of existence. Thus, the Wormp Universe marches onward—a living, breathing epic where every regenerative glow, every cosmic convergence, and every heroic act is part of an endless saga of mystery, unity, and eternal wonder. The story continues to evolve, inviting each new generation to add their chapter to this remarkable chronicle of life, light, and the ceaseless dance of creation. Long after the initial arrival of the Wormps—those enigmatic, regenerative creatures brought to Earth by mysterious aliens—the world has continued to change in ways both wondrous and unsettling. Rooted in the epic narrative of ancient wars, uneasy truces, and the everlasting guardianship of the Four Masters, a new series of events now unfolds that echoes the spirit of the original tale while expanding its cosmic horizon. The Echoes of the First Arrival In the quiet moments before dawn, when the air is thick with the whisper of ages past, villagers near long-forgotten caves still speak of the first Wormps that graced the Earth. These primordial creatures, with their black-hole-like mouths and uncanny ability to regenerate their lives over decades, have become symbols of nature’s indomitable spirit. Yet beneath this serene veneer, hints of a hidden legacy stir. In isolated regions, remnants of alien technology—rusted, cryptic devices left behind by the architects of the Wormps—begin to pulse with latent energy. Some believe these relics hold secrets to even greater powers, suggesting that the Wormps’ true origins are far more intricate than anyone had dared imagine. The Unseen War of Shadows As peace settled after the third great war—a monumental battle that saw humans and Wormps united against a returning alien menace—new tensions have begun to emerge. In the deep, ember-lit corridors of the volcanic Ember Wastes, murmurs speak of a covert uprising. A faction of Wormps, long hidden in the darkness and nurtured by forbidden energies, now stirs with a quiet determination. These “Forgotten Wormps”—with elemental abilities that echo the ancient, lost potential of their kind—are not driven by malice but by a desire to reclaim a power that was once nearly lost. Their subtle movements have started to unsettle the balance, and even the wise elders among the Wormp Tamers have noted the tremors of an unseen war that could disrupt the fragile harmony carefully maintained since the last cosmic conflict. The Emergence of the Celestial Covenant In response to these new undercurrents, secret orders that have always straddled the line between myth and reality are mobilizing once again. The ancient Wormp Tamers, whose wisdom was passed down through generations in whispered lore and sacred rituals, have formed a renewed alliance with human scholars and warriors. This Celestial Covenant aims not only to protect the legacy of the Wormps but also to uncover the hidden truths buried in alien relics and cosmic runes. Among their ranks, a young yet resolute leader—rumored to be a direct descendant of those who once brokered peace in the Third War—rallies a new generation to restore order. His bond with a particularly vibrant Wormp, whose regenerative glow now seems to pulse in time with the heartbeat of the Earth, stands as a beacon of hope amid emerging uncertainty. Whispers of a New Prophecy In the shadow of these stirring events, an ancient prophecy long confined to the secret scrolls of the Wormp Tamers is finally being spoken of in hushed tones. It tells of a time when the celestial alignments would once again converge, and when the combined forces of the forgotten and the beloved would awaken to either herald a new age of enlightenment—or plunge the world into an era of chaos. This prophecy, echoing the themes of creation and destruction found in the earliest
legends, speaks of a convergence point at which the essence of the original Wormps might be unlocked anew, setting in motion events that could redefine the very fabric of the universe. The prophecy warns that while the Four Masters remain unassailable in their power, the latent energies hidden within the wormp lineage and the relics left by their alien creators might one day challenge the balance—if only momentarily. The Unyielding Guardians and the Road Ahead Yet, through all these unfolding mysteries and the murmur of a hidden uprising, the eternal presence of the Four Masters—Wormy, Dragoon, Ninja Master, and Rainbow Bunny—casts an unbreakable shadow of order and protection. Their silent vigilance reassures the world that no matter how tumultuous the tides of change may become, there exists an unyielding light to guide both humans and Wormps alike. As the Celestial Covenant prepares for a journey into the unknown and the Forgotten Wormps stir beneath the surface, the narrative of this universe continues to evolve, blending the raw intensity of ancient wars with the gentle promise of renewal. In this ever-deepening saga, the legacy of the original story endures. Every new alliance, every hidden relic, and every whispered prophecy is woven into a grand tapestry that honors the past while boldly facing the future. It is a story of transformation—where fear gives way to understanding, and where even the most inconspicuous of creatures can hold the key to cosmic destiny. The Wormp Universe marches forward into a new dawn, guided by the enduring light of its eternal masters and the unbreakable bonds of a world that refuses to forget its origins. The Role of the Mormps in the First and Second Human-Wormp Wars Background on Mormps The Mormps, unlike the versatile and (mostly) cooperative Wormps, are solitary and aggressive predators native to Zendoria’s most hostile regions—the Scorching Ravines, the Toxic Marshes, and the Hollow Gale Peaks. They have a deep-seated hatred for Wormps, attacking them on sight. Some scholars believe this animosity is ancient, rooted in the fact that Mormps were an earlier, flawed creation by the Zen'Korah, discarded in favor of the more stable Wormps. This origin theory is unconfirmed but widely accepted among Wormp historians. The First Human-Wormp War (Year 0-7 AGW [After Great War]) At the dawn of the first great Human-Wormp conflict, Mormps played a chaotic but pivotal role. As humanity waged war against the increasingly unpredictable and hungry Wormps, the Mormps saw an opportunity for widespread slaughter. They emerged from their desolate territories in swarms, attacking both sides indiscriminately. The Mormp Onslaught (Year 2 AGW): Midway through the war, as human armies clashed with Wormp swarms in the Verdant Plains, a massive horde of Mormps descended from the Hollow Gale Peaks. These feral creatures devastated both forces, spreading chaos. Their poison-coated fangs and claws infected entire battalions, while Wind Mormps created deadly tornadoes that tore through settlements. No sides were spared. The Truce of Desperation (Year 3 AGW): Briefly, humans and Wormp Tamers forged an uneasy ceasefire to repel the Mormp invasion. Together, they drove the creatures back into their dark territories, though many regions remained contaminated by Mormp Poison Worms, burrowing beneath the soil. Aftermath: Once the Mormps were repelled, hostilities between Wormps and humans resumed, but the scars left by the Mormp onslaught lingered. The ravaged lands became known as the Blighted Front, a cursed no-man’s-land where neither Wormp nor human dared to tread for decades. The Second Human-Wormp War (Year 50-56 AGW)
By the time the second great war erupted, both humans and Wormps had advanced in strategy and strength. Yet the Mormps remained a wild card—feral, unpredictable, and hungry for destruction. The Mormp Cults (Year 52 AGW): Desperate for power, rogue human factions and exiled Wormp Tamers turned to the Mormps. They began worshiping them as avatars of destruction, forming dangerous Mormp Cults. These cultists captured and bred Mormps, unleashing them as living weapons against their enemies. This act horrified both the Human Alliance and the Wormp Clans. The Siege of Black Hollow (Year 54 AGW): A Mormp Cult, riding massive Earth Mormps, laid siege to Black Hollow, a major human fortress. Poison Mormps tainted the water supply, and Wind Mormps brought screaming gales that tore defenders from the walls. Only the intervention of the Rainbow Bunny, one of the Four Masters, ended the siege by banishing the Mormps into a pocket dimension for a century. The Mormp Exile (Year 56 AGW): Toward the war's end, a grand ritual led by Ninja Master sealed large Mormp populations into ancient ruins deep beneath Zendoria. Though they are believed trapped, sightings of rogue Mormps persist—suggesting the seals may one day break. Mormps' Legacy To this day, Mormps remain a symbol of Zendoria’s most primal violence. They are neither ally nor foe in the traditional sense—just pure hostility. The events of the first two wars cemented their reputation as agents of destruction, feared by both Wormps and humans alike. In whispers, some say they wait…for the Fourth Great War. Zendoria’s massive history, sprawling continents, and multiple civilizations would naturally have lots of different ways to track time. Some are ancient, tied to cosmic events, others are more recent, used for history keeping, wars, or important cultural shifts. Here’s a breakdown of timekeeping systems in Zendoria, including AGW and others. Some are official, some are cultural, and some are region-specific. 1. AGW (After Great War) What it tracks: Time starting after the First Great War, the first massive conflict between Humans and Wormps. Origin: Instituted by human scholars and Wormp Tamers to mark the start of a new, uneasy peace and the modern era. Common Usage: Most widely used in human civilizations, historical records, diplomacy, and Wormp Tamer guilds. Example Year: 75 AGW means 75 years after the First Great War ended. 2. BGW (Before Great War) What it tracks: The time period before the First Great War between Humans and Wormps. Usage: Mostly used by historians and scholars when referencing ancient civilizations, Wormp legends, or pre-war human kingdoms. Example Year: 232 BGW means 232 years before the Great War began. 3. YOM (Years of Mormp) What it tracks: Time used by the Mormp Cults and rogue factions. It starts from when Mormp Cults first rose to power during the Second Human-Wormp War. Philosophy: They believe the age of destruction begins with the Mormps. Every year is seen as a countdown to "The Return of the Mormp King." Example Year: Year 13 YOM would be 13 years since the cults began their uprising. Who uses it: Fringe groups, Mormp Cults, doomsayers. 4. CY (Celestial Years) What it tracks: Time based on the cycles of Zendoria's three moons: Veyra, Illun, and Marrox.
Use: Preferred by Wormp Elders, Arcane Nomads, and the Celestial Covenant who believe the moons influence Wormp life energy and elemental surges. Every full cycle of all three moons aligns once every 128 Zendoria days, marking a Celestial Year. Example Year: 4th Cycle, 89th Celestial Year. Interesting Fact: Some Wormp evolutions are said to only happen at specific Celestial alignments. 5. TDK (Time of the Drifted Kingdoms) What it tracks: Time period used by the ancient Floating Cities (like Aetheria), starting from when their cities detached from the surface world. Use: Common in sky civilizations, including floating kingdoms, sky pirates, and the Rainbow Bunny's followers. Measurement: Time is based on the rotation of sky currents rather than the planet itself. Days and years pass differently in sky realms. Example Year: Year 571 TDK (571 years since the cities rose). Fun Fact: This time system is faster; one TDK year is about 9 AGW months. 6. ZAE (Zen’Korah Ascension Era) What it tracks: Ancient Zen'Korah time, starting from when they first arrived in Zendoria and began their experiments. Use: Found mostly in ruins, relics, and ancient data crystals. Few can read or interpret ZAE time. Example Year: ZAE 130,041 (130,041 years after Zen'Korah landed). Interesting Fact: The Forgotten Wormps and some ancient Wormp temples still use ZAE calendars in hidden chambers. 7. LW (Lunar Watch) What it tracks: Short cycles used by travelers and nomads, based on the waxing and waning of Zendoria’s largest moon, Veyra. Use: Common among desert tribes, sea navigators, and Wormp Tamers on long journeys. Each Lunar Watch lasts 14 days, and there are 26 Watches in a standard AGW year. Example: "The 12th Lunar Watch of the Silver Moon." 8. TW (Time of Wormp) What it tracks: Spiritual time based on the life cycles of Ancient Wormps, especially the regenerating "Three Lives" system Wormps have. Use: Rituals, prophecies, and Wormp Life Festivals. **Each TW cycle is 30 years, representing a Wormp’s regeneration period before regaining a lost life. Example Year: "Second Life, 14th Year of TW"—meaning 14 years into a Wormp’s second life cycle. Fun Fact: Some Wormp villages plant Rebirth Trees during each TW cycle to symbolize regrowth. How These Time Systems Interact AGW/BGW is the most universal calendar—used in politics, warfare, and trade. Celestial Years and Lunar Watches guide Wormp behavior and farming. TDK and ZAE are rarer but used in sky kingdoms and ancient ruins. YOM is mostly feared, used by cults who believe Mormps will bring the end. TW cycles inspire legends about Wormp immortality and rebirth, especially during major festivals. Timekeeping Devices Chrono-Orbs: Floating spheres that shift colors based on the current time system. Moon Dials: Ancient stone structures used to track Celestial Years. The Drift Clock: A massive, floating clock tower in Aetheria that follows TDK time.
The Spiral Calendar: Found in Zen’Korah ruins, this scroll-like artifact measures ZAE time in galactic rotations. Wormy (God of Wormps, Dogs, Ghosts, and Time) Strength: Wormy can easily lift over 10000^110000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria. His strength allows him to manipulate entire planets and celestial bodies without breaking a sweat, bending the universe’s physical laws. Speed: Wormy can move at 900,871,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000+ miles per yoctosecond with ease—speed beyond comprehension, able to traverse vast stretches of space and time instantaneously, and even move through time itself. Other Abilities: Time Manipulation: Wormy controls time itself—slowing it, accelerating it, reversing it, or even stopping it completely. He can reshape entire timelines, alter past events, and control the flow of temporal energy. Regeneration: Instant and infinite regeneration—Wormy can heal from anything, no matter how catastrophic. His regenerative abilities extend across timelines and reality itself. Cosmic Influence: The ability to summon and control Wormps, ghosts, and celestial beings. Wormy’s reach transcends dimensions, giving him influence over the very essence of existence. Reality Warping: Wormy can manipulate the fabric of reality, altering the structure of the universe to suit his needs, making him an overwhelming force. Ninja Master (God of Weapons, Speed, and Intelligence) Strength: Ninja Master can lift over 10^10000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria, with his physical strength enhanced by his mental acuity and mastery over weapons. His strength allows him to strike with precision and force that could shatter even the toughest of beings. Speed: Ninja Master is capable of moving at 90,870,000,000,000,000,000 miles per yoctosecond, able to perform actions and reactions faster than thought itself. His speed transcends physical limitations, allowing him to move through space and time at unimaginable rates. Other Abilities: Weapon Mastery: Mastery over any weapon, whether ancient or advanced. Ninja Master can manipulate energy-based or physical weapons with supreme precision. Strategic Genius: With lightning-fast intellect, Ninja Master can outthink any opponent. His mind works faster than even his physical movements, allowing him to predict and counter any attack. Agility and Stealth: His speed and agility are unmatched, and he can move undetected, bypassing even the most advanced defenses. Dragoon (God of Strength, Fire, Dragons, and Power) Strength: Dragoon can lift over 10^10000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria. His brute force and elemental control grant him strength that can crush mountains, level cities, and reshape the very land itself with a single blow. Speed: Dragoon moves at 90,870,000,000,000,000,000 miles per yoctosecond, able to traverse the skies and battle at speeds that rival the speed of light. His dragon-like agility and power allow him to strike with immense force and grace. Other Abilities: Elemental Control (Fire): Dragoon can summon and control vast fires, capable of engulfing entire landscapes in searing flames. His fire manipulation extends to lava and molten energy, which he can unleash in devastating waves. Dragon Mastery: He commands dragons, calling upon these mighty creatures as allies in battle, or even transforming into a dragon-like being himself to enhance his power.
Immense Durability: Dragoon’s physical resilience makes him impervious to most attacks. His body is nearly indestructible, allowing him to withstand blows that would annihilate lesser beings. Rainbow Bunny (God of Rainbows, Color, Happiness, and the Sky) Strength: Rainbow Bunny can lift over 10^10000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria. Her strength stems from her ability to harness the infinite spectrum of light and emotional energy, turning them into powerful forces that shape the world. Speed: Rainbow Bunny moves at 90,870,000,000,000,000,000 miles per yoctosecond, gliding through space and time with ease. Her movements are as fluid as light itself, capable of instantaneous transitions across realms. Other Abilities: Light Manipulation: Rainbow Bunny can control every aspect of light, creating beams of concentrated energy, forming constructs of pure light, and controlling illusions to confuse or overpower her enemies. Emotional Influence: She can manipulate emotions, spreading happiness, joy, and calm or using fear, sorrow, and despair to subdue opponents. Her powers allow her to control and influence the hearts of others. Dimensional Travel: Rainbow Bunny can shift between dimensions and realms with ease, escaping danger or manipulating time and space to create her own advantage. Healing and Protection: Through the power of light and love, she can heal others and shield them from harm, making her an unstoppable force in battles of protection and recovery. Summary of Powers: Wormy: Time manipulation, reality warping, cosmic strength, regeneration, and infinite speed. Ninja Master: Weapon mastery, strategic genius, unparalleled speed, and stealth. Dragoon: Raw physical power, elemental control (fire), dragon mastery, and immense durability. Rainbow Bunny: Light manipulation, emotional influence, dimensional travel, and healing. All Four Masters share the capability of lifting over 10^10000 trillion times the weight of Zendoria and can move at speeds of 90,870,000,000,000,000,000 miles per yoctosecond, making them unstoppable forces. Each has domain over a different aspect of the universe, and together, they maintain the balance of Zendoria. And wormy is the strongest out of them all. At AGW Year 57, a new threat emerges, its called, Pokra, scientists still haven't fully figure out what cause this to happen, but it rumored to be a mutated breed under extreme radiation activity that combines the mormps and other element causing a new specie to appear, Pokras look like wormps, but, just different, it have creepy looking hands that look like a mantis's hand, it sharp, they use it to poke stuff to attack, they can spit out venom that both hurt an enemy and slow them down by a decent amount, the venom can kill a average healthy adult human in around 1 minute and 30 second, but humans already have technology that could help but the venoms are still very, very deadly. Pokras are enemy of the wormp & humans, and are semi-friendly with the mormps, I said they are semi friendly because they also fight each other, Pokras are consider Apex predators and eat nearly any living organism (except those who are very strong, including the four masters, of course) the current number of Pokras are unknown, but it estimated to be around 2 billion, and 400 million, it increasing in a rapid rate, this caused human and wormps to some time team up and hunt down them (human and wormps also do this sometime with the mormps) this group is called HWD (Human Wormp Defender) This is an entity that protect both wormps and humans, formed a few years after the first Pokra attack, known as "Ashfall Day" the Pokras attacked the Human capital and the humans and wormps fight back, after 2 billions of casualties from both sides (Human & Wormps,
v.s Pokras, all three combine) And 15 days of battle, Pokras finally retreat, the second time they attacked (about 3 month later) although humans and wormps are more ready, but this time they teamed up with the mormps, causing 5 billions of casualties and 27 days of battle, the humans and the wormps won again, Pokras have been quite since than, but rumors suggest that they and the mormps, are planning an brutal battle against the human and the wormps. New Region: Liminal Sea — a floating ocean of liquid memory between sky and earth, can extract “memory fish” to craft sound-based artifacts. Three New Factions — Glass Cocoon Guild, Choir of the Deep Silence, Myco-Tide Alliance. New Wormp Types — Crystal-Thread, Mist-Spore, Shellback, Echoform (can learn and mimic voice-pattern skills). New Energy Discipline — Resonance Arts (sound × minerals × Wormp micro-network) + “Pulse Frequency” gear system. New Natural Materials — Starlight Adhesive, Hearstone, Pulse Amber, Tide-Iron, Mist-Down Fungus. Dynamic Seasons — 3-month “Echo Cycle” changes terrain and ecosystem behavior. Massive Landmark Dungeons — Skystring Tree, Inverted Vault, Moonscar Chasm, Coral-Song Graveyard, Feathered Bastion. World Events — Border Expo, Festival of Seeking Voices, Cocoon-Split Tide, Tide-Iron Wars. New Gameplay Modules — Resonance Tuning, Wormp breeding & symbiotic professions, Voiceprint Exploration, Guild Contract Board. Epic Main Storyline (3 Arcs) — The Lost First Voice, The Cocoon Schism, Return to the Pulse Sea. 1. New Regions / Biomes Liminal Sea — liquid memory layer floating in mid-air; fishing yields “memory fish” for sound-print crafting. Moonscar Chasm — cold-light flows at night; cliffs contain Hearstone veins amplifying resonance arts. Mist Cocoon Grove — trees grow hatchable mist cocoons; harvest “Mist-Down Fungus” to make stealth cloaks. Tide-Iron Salt Flats — magnetic metal plates revealed at low tide; vehicles can magnet-glide. Feathered Plateau — wind-slice corridors carry sound; can “wind-record” distant conversations for quests. 2. Factions & Cultures Glass Cocoon Guild — masters of transparent cocoon-glass architecture & gearcraft; controls trade routes. Choir of the Deep Silence — multi-part harmony spellcasters; ranks based on harmony difficulty. Myco-Tide Alliance — fungal network messengers; leaders in agriculture and medicine. Echo-Weavers — mapmakers using woven resonance lines to create “sound maps” of terrain. Tide-Iron Cartel — controls magnetic transport; power struggles over key resources. 3. New Wormp Species & Interactions Crystal-Thread Wormp — crystalline fibers that conduct sound; can act as “strings” for devices. Mist-Spore Wormp — emits camouflage mist, leaves short-lived decoy projections. Shellback Wormp — hardened shell armor, supports gear attachments. Echoform Wormp — learns & mimics player/NPC “voiceprint skills” for a limited time. Cocoon Merge Event (rare) — multiple Wormps fuse temporarily into a giant form for transport/combat/construction. Care & Growth — manage “cocoon warmth,” “pulse frequency,” and “feeding recipes” to improve bond and specialization.
Symbiosis Skill Trees — Defense (Shell), Mobility (Slide), Sensing (Hearing), Support (Tuning), Offense (Shock). 4. Energy & Gear Systems Resonance Arts — combine sound notes + materials to cast healing, movement boosts, barriers, detection. Pulse-Frequency Modules — gear slots: “Main Frequency,” “Sub-Frequency,” “Nodes” for build variety. Voiceprint Recording — collect environmental sounds → compose music phrases → solve puzzles or open ancient locks. Anti-Tuning / Dissonance — sabotaging enemy resonance to weaken abilities or cause chaos in combat. New Lands & Phenomena The Liminal Sea — a drifting ocean of liquid memory suspended between sky and earth; travelers who fall in resurface speaking with voices from centuries past. The Moonscar Chasm — a deep wound in the land where moonlight behaves like water, pooling in cracks and flowing uphill. Mist Cocoon Grove — a silent forest where pale cocoons sway in invisible wind; some contain living Wormps, others… something else. Tide-Iron Salt Flats — magnetic storms cause the ground to hum, drawing in lost compasses, metal relics, and the bones of ships that never saw the sea. Feathered Plateau — the wind here carries whispers from distant lands; locals say the plateau is listening, and one day, it will speak back. 2. Civilizations & Cultures Glass Cocoon Guild — artisans who weave unbreakable translucent shells into everything from towers to tombs. They guard a vault containing the First Cocoon, origin of the Wormps. Choir of the Deep Silence — a wandering order who communicate entirely through harmonic resonance, claiming speech is a “corruption” of truth. Myco-Tide Alliance — not entirely human — their bodies are symbiotic with a vast underground fungal network. They can “dream together” in shared hallucinations. Echo-Weavers — navigators who knot threads soaked in sound to map the geography of memories, not places. Tide-Iron Cartel — magnetic harvesters who believe the earth’s “heartbeat” is slowing — and they’re determined to restart it, no matter the cost. 3. New Wormp Variants (and the questions they raise) Crystal-Thread Wormp — translucent bodies lined with fiber that vibrates when danger is near. But they sometimes hum without cause. Mist-Spore Wormp — uses living mist to hide itself — but some wander into villages and mimic the faces of the dead. Shellback Wormp — their shells carry intricate etchings no one can translate; older shells seem to match star patterns. Echoform Wormp — can perfectly imitate any voice — sometimes including voices never heard before in Zendoria. Convergent Cocoon — once in a century, many Wormps weave into one — and the result is not always alive. 4. Natural Mysteries & Oddities
Pulse Amber — fossilized resonance trapped in golden resin; when cracked, it sings — but only for those it “remembers.” Hearstones — rocks that “listen” and subtly shift shape according to the stories told near them. Mist-Down Fungus — soft, pale fungi that bloom where reality feels “thinner,” often at the edges of dreams. The Starlight Adhesive — sticky residue left by a falling star that can bind anything — except shadow. Resonance Wells — pools that ripple in perfect circles, even without wind; looking too long into one risks hearing your own voice beg for help. 5. Recurring World Events The Border Expo — where cultures exchange relics they don’t understand, hoping someone else might. The Seeking Voices Festival — when people open their doors to any wandering sound, hoping it’s an ancestor returned. The Cocoon-Split Tide — when the moon’s pull causes cocoons to unravel across the land, revealing whatever’s inside — alive, dead, or stranger. The Iron Wars — a series of skirmishes over magnetic veins; every battle fought near them ends in eerie silence. 6. Questions the World Asks Why do Wormps mimic voices of people who’ve never existed? What was the First Cocoon, and what broke it? Why do certain places hum only when no one is watching? Is the “heartbeat” of Zendoria really slowing, or is it breathing in? Who built the glass vaults in the desert that open only during storms? 1. Strange Lands Beyond the Map The Veil-Edge — a horizon of shimmering air; travelers who pass through return months older with no memory, clutching objects that don’t belong to this world. The Ash-Rain Bluffs — where it “snows” with soft gray ash, even in midsummer; the ash burns only if you speak while it falls. Blackwater Orchard — trees grow fruit that tastes different depending on your deepest regret; some refuse to eat twice. Mirrorstep Hollow — a valley where puddles reflect events from years in the future — but never in order. The Bleached Wilds — an area where all color fades; Wormps here are albino, and their cocoons are as fragile as paper. 2. Mythic & Half-Believed Beings The First Voice — a presence said to have sung Zendoria into existence; Choir of the Deep Silence claims it still hums beneath the Pulse Sea. The Lantern-Strain — bioluminescent Wormps said to guide the dead to the “Listening Shore.” The Glass Shepherd — a figure glimpsed only in reflections, herding flocks of invisible creatures through abandoned cities. The Nine-Tongued Shade — a parasitic shadow that speaks in nine different voices at once; it offers directions, always leading to ruin. The Sky-Bound Leviathan — massive shapes moving above the clouds; their shadows pass across the land in absolute silence. 3. Forbidden Places
The Vault Below Veilspire — guarded by the Glass Cocoon Guild; locals claim the doors whisper names at night. The Cathedral of Pulse — a hollow mountain filled with stone “organ pipes” that play by themselves on certain moon phases. The Archive of Forgotten Seasons — a ruin where entire weather patterns are stored in crystalline jars. The Split Loom — a tangle of massive roots woven with silver thread; cutting it is said to erase one memory forever. The Chasm of Unheard Cries — a canyon you can see but never hear; all sound vanishes inside. 4. Living Phenomena Echo-Bloom Tides — every thirty years, the shores glow with bioluminescent waves that hum in harmony; some say it’s a mating call between continents. Pulse Storms — lightning strikes that carry no light, only a deep vibration through the ground; survivors say they hear “footsteps” afterward. Cocoon Drifts — masses of unclaimed cocoons floating down rivers; most are empty, some move. Memory-Fog — a low mist that causes travelers to forget their destination but not their journey. Glassfall — rain of sharp crystal shards that melt on touch; often leaves geometric scars in the ground. 5. Unsettling Truths & Rumors Wormps avoid certain songs, even when raised from birth to hear them. A Hearstone in the southern plains grew a human ear overnight. Myco-Tide elders can identify someone’s birthplace by tasting their tears. Every Glass Cocoon Guildmaster disappears within a week of retirement. In the last century, five moons have been sighted; there are only supposed to be two. 6. Loose Threads for Storytelling A Cocoon-Split reveals a Wormp made entirely of light — but it casts two shadows. The Choir of the Deep Silence is missing one harmonic note, and no one remembers how it sounds. A crystal shell fragment bears the face of a living person — but they’ve never been to Zendoria. The Moonscar Chasm deepens by exactly one hand-span every year. A child draws maps of lands no one can find, but every Wormp migrates exactly as she predicts. 1. The Veil-Edge A shimmering horizon where sky and land seem to melt into each other, forming a wavering wall of air that ripples like heat haze. Those who cross vanish from sight in moments, reappearing days, months, or years later with no recollection of where they have been. They often return holding objects made from materials unknown anywhere in Zendoria — glass that bends sound, wood that bleeds ink, coins stamped with moons no one has seen. Maps cannot chart the Veil-Edge; it shifts unpredictably, sometimes hugging the borders of known lands, sometimes curling into the very heart of a city. The Myco-Tide Alliance believes it is the skin of the world itself, flexing as Zendoria breathes. 2. The Ash-Rain Bluffs Cliffs of pale stone where, without warning, soft gray ash begins to fall like snow, covering the land in a delicate, muffling layer. It clings to skin and clothing with a faint static hum. The ash is harmless if one stays silent — but if you speak, it sears the tongue and blackens the teeth. Legends say the ash is the cremated dust of ancient Wormp cocoons, released when the wind stirs certain cliff hollows. The Choir of the Deep Silence conducts annual pilgrimages here, believing the ash holds echoes of the First Voice, and that the pain it inflicts on speech is a form of purification.
3. Blackwater Orchard A grove of gnarled, black-barked trees that grow only along the edge of stagnant, dark ponds. Their fruit appears in many colors, but the taste is never the same twice — it changes to reflect the eater’s most painful regret. For some, it is unbearably bitter; for others, it is unbearably sweet. Locals claim eating two fruits from the same tree will let you taste someone else’s regret, and some use this as a form of truth-seeking. The Orchard is avoided during the Echo Cycle, when the ponds churn without wind, and whispers in voices not your own call from between the trees. 4. Mirrorstep Hollow A sunken valley where every puddle reflects not the present, but moments from years — even decades — in the future. These glimpses are not in sequence, and their accuracy is fiercely debated. The Hollow is littered with travelers’ discarded possessions, abandoned in panic after seeing something they could not accept. Some factions mine the reflective water into flasks, using it for divination, though the water always evaporates after a single viewing. No animal native to Zendoria will step into the Hollow, and even Wormps strain against their bonds when led here. 5. The Bleached Wilds A vast expanse where all color fades to pale shades of white and gray, as if the world itself has been drained. Flowers bloom in ghostly hues, and even blood spills as clear liquid. Wormps here are albino, with translucent cocoons so fragile they disintegrate in a whisper of powder. Travelers claim the Wilds are not silent, but muted — every sound seems to come from far away. The Glass Cocoon Guild maintains an outpost at the Wilds’ edge, studying the region’s unusual minerals, which, when taken elsewhere, slowly regain their color — but never their original shade. 6. The Lantern Marsh A sprawling wetland where phosphorescent moss grows thick over the water, glowing with a pale green light. At night, glowing Wormps — the Lantern-Strain — float just above the waterline, casting long, rippling shadows. They are said to guide lost travelers, though not always to safety. Some have followed a Lantern Wormp for days, only to arrive at a place they’ve never seen on any map. The Myco-Tide Alliance believes the marsh is a bridge between the living and the dead, and during the Festival of Seeking Voices, mourners wade in to ask the Lanterns to deliver messages. 7. The Boneglass Dunes Rolling desert sands laced with pale crystal shards sharp enough to flay skin. The wind carries an eerie chime as the shards strike each other, and in certain places, the dunes “sing” — low, vibrating notes that can be felt in the bones. Miners seek the shards for their unmatched resonance in crafting Pulse-Frequency instruments, but digging too deep can unearth strange shapes beneath the sand: smooth, white structures like colossal ribs, fused with the crystal. Some believe the entire desert is the skeleton of a leviathan from before Zendoria had seas. 8. The Whisperroot Canopy A towering forest where the roots of the trees arch above ground, twisting into hollow corridors large enough for caravans to pass through. Wind funneling through these root-tunnels creates a constant low murmur, said to be the trees speaking to each other. The Echo-Weavers claim to have translated fragments of this language, which speaks of coming storms and migrations of Wormps. Those who sleep in the Canopy often wake to find intricate root carvings beside their bedding — carvings depicting scenes from the sleeper’s own past, even ones they have long forgotten. 9. The Starfallen Steppe A flat expanse scattered with massive craters lined in silver glass, where meteorites have struck over centuries. At night, the ground glows faintly, as if reflecting a sky from some other world. Starlight Adhesive seeps from some of the craters, forming slow-moving pools that can trap
shadows in their surface. These “shadow pools” are considered dangerous — if your shadow is taken, you lose the ability to dream until it is returned. Wanderers report seeing tall, angular silhouettes walking along the Steppe during moonless nights, never leaving footprints. 10. The Coral-Song Graveyard An inland reef of fossilized coral towers, stretching for miles, left behind when the sea withdrew centuries ago. The wind passing through the coral spires creates haunting, multi-layered harmonies that shift with the seasons. Some of the towers are hollow, housing colonies of small, translucent Wormps that pulse in time with the music. During certain tides, water floods the Graveyard again, and the music deepens, resonating through the bones of anyone nearby. Scholars debate whether the “song” is simply wind and tide, or if the coral itself is remembering the ocean. 11. The Inkvein Delta A labyrinth of black-water channels and pale sandbars where the current stains everything it touches in deep indigo. The “ink” is not waterborne sediment but the secretions of massive, unseen creatures burrowed into the riverbed. Locals bottle the ink as a permanent dye, but some warn that writing with it invites the creatures to “read” you in return. Wormps here often have dark, flowing patterns on their skin, mirroring the shifting river currents. 12. The Cloudpiercer Cliffs Mountains so tall their upper peaks vanish into permanent cloudbanks, lit from within by strange flickers of green light. Flocks of winged Wormps nest along the vertical rock faces, weaving cocoons into the cliffside that sway in the wind like lanterns. The Cartel believes the green light is from magnetic storms generated by ore seams in the peaks, but the Choir claims it is “skyfire” from the breath of the Sky-Bound Leviathans. 13. Hollowlight Ravine A narrow canyon lined with mineral walls that absorb sunlight all day, then emit a faint glow at night. Travelers here report seeing shadows move against the walls in slow, deliberate patterns, as if trying to communicate. The Echo-Weavers once mapped the shadows’ movements, but the patterns changed every season, making the chart useless. It’s said that standing still for too long in the Ravine will cause your own shadow to begin moving independently. 14. The Rainswept Tangle A dense jungle where it rains nearly every day, but the rain never reaches the ground. It hangs suspended among the massive overlapping leaves, forming glistening pools in midair. Travelers must climb through dripping corridors, careful not to disturb the suspended water — a single splash can awaken hidden predators that swim invisibly through the rain itself. The Myco-Tide Alliance gathers rare fungi here that grow only on leaves that have held water for a full year without spilling. 15. The Frostspire Expanse A frozen plain dominated by a singular black spire of ice that never melts, even under direct sunlight. Inside the spire is a spiraling tunnel lined with smooth, glassy walls that distort sound into strange chords. Stories say that if you hum a perfect note inside, the spire will “answer” with a voice from your future. Several expeditions to reach the heart of the spire have returned missing days from their journals, with pages torn or replaced by writing in unfamiliar hands. 16. The Crimson Loom A canyon of red stone where fine crimson dust floats perpetually in the air, weaving intricate patterns before settling on the ground. The dust stains everything it touches, even skin, for months. Some believe the patterns are coded messages from the earth itself, but they dissolve if recorded in ink. At night, the canyon glows faintly as the dust catches moonlight, making the entire chasm look like a living tapestry.
17. The Dreamer’s Wreath An island of luminous flowers arranged in perfect spirals, each bloom opening only when someone is watching. Those who fall asleep within the Wreath dream with impossible clarity — dreams so real they leave marks on the body when one wakes. Several explorers have vanished entirely here, their bedrolls surrounded by newly opened flowers with human-shaped impressions in the petals. 18. The Hollow Crown An extinct volcano whose rim has been carved into thousands of archways, each facing a different point on the horizon. Wind through the arches produces different tones, and locals say that standing in the correct arch on the correct day will open a “view” into another place entirely. The Glass Cocoon Guild attempted to chart all the tones, but abandoned the project after their lead cartographer claimed the arches began whispering his name. 19. The Mourning Stair A stone staircase carved into the side of a mountain, rising for miles and disappearing into mist. At every landing sits a weathered statue, each one depicting a figure mid-scream. The steps are worn but never dusty, as if countless feet climb them daily — yet no one is ever seen ascending or descending. Those who reach the top (few ever do) claim there is no summit, only another staircase continuing upward into darkness. 20.
